CheckTitle: Cloud Functions are not publicly invocable
Description: Ensures that no Cloud Function has IAM bindings granting invocation rights to 'allUsers' or 'allAuthenticatedUsers'. Functions should only be callable by explicitly authorized identities or services.
Risk: Publicly accessible Cloud Functions can be invoked by any internet user or any authenticated Google account. This exposes business logic, enables abuse (e.g., denial-of-wallet via high invocation costs), and can lead to unauthorized data access or code execution if the function processes sensitive operations.

Code.CLI:
gcloud functions remove-iam-policy-binding <FUNCTION_NAME> \
  --region=<REGION> \
  --member='allUsers' \
  --role='roles/cloudfunctions.invoker'

gcloud functions remove-iam-policy-binding <FUNCTION_NAME> \
  --region=<REGION> \
  --member='allAuthenticatedUsers' \
  --role='roles/cloudfunctions.invoker'
Code.Other:
1. Go to Google Cloud Console > Cloud Functions.
2. Select the function.
3. Open the Permissions tab.
4. Remove any binding with 'allUsers' or 'allAuthenticatedUsers'.
5. Grant invocation rights only to specific service accounts or user groups.
Code.Terraform:
```hcl
# Do NOT include allUsers or allAuthenticatedUsers as members
resource "google_cloudfunctions2_function_iam_binding" "invoker" {
  project        = "<project_id>"
  location       = "<region>"
  cloud_function = google_cloudfunctions2_function.example.name
  role           = "roles/cloudfunctions.invoker"
  members        = ["serviceAccount:<sa>@<project>.iam.gserviceaccount.com"]
}
```

Recommendation.Text: Remove all IAM bindings that grant 'allUsers' or 'allAuthenticatedUsers' invocation access to Cloud Functions. Use service-account-based authentication for internal callers, and Cloud Endpoints or API Gateway with authentication for externally exposed functions.

Notes: This check evaluates function-level IAM policies. Organization policy constraints/iam.allowedPolicyMemberDomains can prevent public bindings at the org level.
